nativeRange.habitat: Mini Bolbitis is a cultivated dwarf trade form. The parent phenotype most commonly associated with the 'Baby Leaf' name is Bolbitis heteroclita 'Difformis', originating from the Philippines, where it grows as a small epiphytic fern on wet rocks and wood at the margins of shaded forest streams. Some vendors market the same trade name as a small-leaf form of the African species Bolbitis heudelotii, and provenance is not consistently reported across the hobby trade.

summary: Mini Bolbitis is a compact dwarf fern sold under the trade name 'Baby Leaf', typically growing to about 5–10 cm. Like other Bolbitis ferns, it attaches to driftwood or rock rather than rooting in substrate, thrives in low to moderate light, and grows slowly without requiring added CO2.
careNotes: Mini Bolbitis 'Baby Leaf' is a smaller-growing trade form of the genus Bolbitis, most consistently identified as the Philippine-origin 'Difformis' cultivar of Bolbitis heteroclita (see [fw-plant-099]), though some vendors market the same or a similar dwarf form as a small-leaf variant of the African Bolbitis heudelotii (see [fw-plant-009]) — provenance and parent species are inconsistently reported across the hobby trade. Aquarium care is the same regardless of lineage: Mini Bolbitis is a slow-growing epiphytic rhizome fern, meaning it draws nutrients from the water column rather than through buried roots. The rhizome (the thick horizontal stem from which the fronds emerge) must be tied or glued to driftwood or porous rock and NEVER buried, as covering the rhizome reliably causes rot. Submerged fronds typically reach only 2–4 inches (about 5–10 cm), making it well suited to foreground or midground attachment in smaller tanks where the full-sized parent species would quickly overgrow the space. Lighting preference is low to moderate, generally around 25–50 PAR with a 6–8 hour photoperiod; intense direct light tends to bleach the delicate fronds or encourage algae growth on the leaves. CO2 supplementation is not required but does meaningfully speed growth in a species that is otherwise quite slow. Placing Mini Bolbitis in an area with moderate to higher flow is generally recommended, as good water movement helps prevent algae from settling on the fronds. Newly imported specimens commonly go through an acclimation 'melt' in which fronds turn transparent and disintegrate before the plant produces new submerged-form growth — this is a normal adjustment response and not a sign of a dying plant.
breedingNotes: Mini Bolbitis is propagated vegetatively by dividing the horizontal rhizome with sharp, sterile scissors. Each division should retain at least two to three growth points (fiddleheads) along with existing fronds and roots, and should be tied or glued to a fresh piece of hardscape until it anchors on its own. It is generally advisable to wait until the parent plant carries 3–4 leaves on each side of the rhizome before splitting, to give each division a healthy start. Adventitious plantlets occasionally appear on mature fronds and can be detached once they carry their own small rhizome and roots. Sexual reproduction by spores is limited to emersed-grown specimens and is essentially unreported for plants kept fully submerged in aquarium culture.

primary.name: Buce Plant
primary.notes: Primary source for scientific identification (Bolbitis heteroclita 'Difformis'), origin (Southeast Asia), submerged size (2-4 inches / 5-10 cm), low lighting, pH (6-7.5), CO2 ('Not necessary'), epiphyte placement, rhizome-burial warning, propagation by rhizome division, and beginner difficulty. Same vendor's blog profile is internally inconsistent about parent species (heudelotii vs heteroclita); we follow the product page's 'heteroclita Difformis' identification as it matches the Philippine 'Difformis' cultivar of fw-plant-099.

additional.name: Terrarium Master — Mini Bolbitis (Baby Leaf)
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (68-77°F = 20-25°C), pH (5.5-7.5), soft water preference, lighting (low to moderate, 25-50 PAR, 6-8 h photoperiod), CO2 optional, epiphyte-on-hardscape placement, propagation by rhizome division (2-3 growth points per cut), water-column feeder (no root feeding), and 'melt' on introduction. Provides the broader pH floor (5.5) and lower temperature floor (20°C) used here.

additional.name: Buce Plant — Plant Profile: Bolbitis heudelotii 'Difformis'
additional.notes: Source of the temperature upper bound (70-80°F = 21-27°C, taken as 27°C max here) and confirmation of low-to-moderate light, 6-8 h photoperiod, soft-water preference, CO2-optional, midground/epiphyte placement, rhizome-never-buried, higher-flow placement to prevent algae, and water-parameter stability sensitivity. Note: this profile attributes the cultivar to Bolbitis heudelotii (Africa), while the same vendor's product page attributes it to Bolbitis heteroclita Difformis (Southeast Asia) — the trade name 'Mini Bolbitis Baby Leaf' is used for both lineages in the hobby.

additional.name: KeepFishKeeping — Mini Bolbitis (Baby Leaf)
additional.notes: Third source: identifies the product as genus Bolbitis 'Native To: Africa' (cultivated at Florida Aquatic Nurseries), with low-to-high light tolerance, slow growth, soft-water preference (tolerates moderate hardness), spreading-rhizome propagation, and epiphyte attachment to driftwood/rock. Used to confirm undemanding/beginner difficulty and the African-lineage trade interpretation noted in habitat.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Bolbitis
additional.notes: Taxonomy fallback: family Dryopteridaceae, order Polypodiales. Confirms that Bolbitis heudelotii normally grows fully submerged while Bolbitis heteroclita grows at water margins and tolerates submersion — relevant to the parent-species ambiguity for this dwarf trade form.

propagation: rhizome division (2-3 growth points per cut)
